title: 10 best herbal creams for benign prostatic hypertrophy

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Puncture vine creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain serenoa repens, an herbal extract that targets the underlying causes of enlarged prostate. This natural ingredient has been shown to reduce inflammation and block the conversion of testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate growth. By addressing these underlying issues, puncture vine creams can help alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination, nocturia, and urinary retention associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of BPH.

Sterols: Sterols, like beta-sitosterol, help block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to benign prostatic hyperplasia, thereby reducing the growth of enlarged prostate tissue.
Phytosterols: Phytosterols, such as campesterol and stigmasterol, have anti-androgenic properties, which help reduce the levels of DHT in the body, thereby slowing down the progression of BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of BPH by reducing inflammation and improving urinary flow.
Rhyzomes: Contain alkaloids that have been shown to relax smooth muscle and improve prostate function, making them effective in managing BPH symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried tribulus terrestris leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested material using shade drying or sun drying methods for at least two weeks to preserve its properties.
Crush the dried tribulus terrestris plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or blender to enhance extraction.
Mix 20g of the powdered tribulus terrestris with 100ml of coconut oil or olive oil in a heatproof glass bowl for a period of one hour, stirring frequently.

Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids and allowing it to cool before storing in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain fatty acids that mimic the effect of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone responsible for prostate gland growth. By blocking DHT's binding to androgen receptors, saw palmetto may help slow or stop the progression of BPH symptoms such as urinary frequency, urgency, and weak stream flow. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with an enlarged prostate. As a result, saw palmetto creams can provide relief from BPH-related symptoms without resorting to pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_properties: Inhibitory activity on 5-alpha-reductase: Saw palmetto cream has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH by causing prostate cell growth and proliferation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which is a common symptom of BPH and can contribute to enlarged prostates.

Antioxidant activity: Saw palmetto cream contains antioxidants that help protect the prostate cells from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to cellular degeneration and enlargement of the prostate.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fatty acids and sterols, which help to reduce prostate inflammation and block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH.
Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant are rich in beta-sitosterol, which has been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to DHT, reducing symptoms of BPH.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried serenoa repens leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered serenoa repens with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 3:7 by weight, adding beeswax at 20% of the total mixture.
Heat the mixture to around 60°c using a double boiler, stirring occasionally for about 30 minutes, until all ingredients are fully incorporated and melted.
Allow the mixture to cool down to around 40°c, then add a preservative such as potassium sorbate at a concentration of 0.1% by weight, mixing well.
Fill sterile glass jars with the final cream formulation, storing them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until consumed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with BPH. The creams may reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, leading to a decrease in prostate size and relief from urinary retention, frequency, and nocturia. Additionally, the creams may also help improve urine flow and reduce symptoms of urinary hesitancy. By addressing these issues, stinging nettle creams can provide natural relief for men experiencing BPH-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle extracts contain compounds that reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of BPH such as enlarged prostate and frequent urination.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in nettle creams help protect the prostate cells from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to BPH development and progression.

Prostaglandin-reducing properties: Nettle extracts contain compounds that inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can stimulate prostatic growth and lead to benign hyperplasia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain a high concentration of steroidal saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and hormone-regulating properties, making them useful in treating benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves and dry them according to the guidelines for drying herbs in a warm dark place for seven days.
Combine one part dried nettle leaves with two parts sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 45 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar removing any excess oil and set it aside to cool completely at room temperature for three hours.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax pellets with 20 grams of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly until melted and smooth within 20 minutes.
Combine the cooled nettle oil mixture with the melted wax and oil mixture and pour it into glass jars allowing it to cool and set at room temperature for three hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the prostate gland. This reduction in inflammation can help alleviate symptoms such as urinary frequency and difficulty starting or maintaining a steady stream of urine. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties may also help protect against cellular damage and promote overall prostate health, making it a promising natural remedy for managing benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
remedy_properties: C curcumin's anti-inflammatory effects: Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of BPH such as urinary frequency and discomfort.

Turmeric's antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage prostate tissue and contribute to BPH development, promoting overall prostate health and reducing oxidative stress.

Curcumin's ability to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase enzyme: Curcumin inhibits the activity of this enzyme, which is responsible for converting testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to BPH growth and symptom development.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in volatile oils, particularly turmerone and atlantone, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that may help relieve prostatic discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh turmeric roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the turmeric roots in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or until they are completely dry.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of the ground turmeric powder with 50g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl and stir well for 2 hours.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until it forms a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zucchini creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because its unique blend of herbal extracts, including saw palmetto and beta-sitosterol, effectively targets enlarged prostate symptoms. These natural compounds have been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH. By reducing DHT levels, zucchini creams may help alleviate frequent urination, weak urine flow, and other common symptoms associated with an enlarged prostate.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Many herbal extracts, such as those found in saw palmetto and beta-sitosterol, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and pressure on the prostate gland, alleviating BPH symptoms.
Hormone regulation: Some herbs like saw palmetto and pygeum bark extract may help regulate hormone imbalances, particularly testosterone levels, which can contribute to BPH development.

Antioxidant activity: Antioxidants present in herbal extracts like lycopene and green tea polyphenols may help protect the prostate gland from oxidative stress and cell damage, which can contribute to BPH progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Zucchini roots contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to help reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle tissues in the prostate, making them useful for alleviating BPH symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the zucchini plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe irritated prostate tissue and reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cucurbita pepo leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated carefully ensuring no contamination or damage.
Dry the harvested cucurbita pepo leaves and stems using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air drying for 24 hours monitoring temperature and humidity.
Grind dried cucurbita pepo leaves and stems into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle extracting the essential oils and active compounds.
Mix 200g of powdered cucurbita pepo with 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler heating until the mixture reaches 70°c while stirring constantly for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before filling glass jars and storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help to reduce swelling in the prostate gland, alleviate symptoms of BPH, and promote overall urinary health. The herbal extracts in these creams also stimulate the body's natural detoxification processes, allowing for the removal of excess hormones that can contribute to prostate enlargement. This holistic approach can provide relief from frequent urination, nocturia, and other common BPH symptoms.
remedy_properties: Saw Palmetto: Saw palmetto may help alleviate BPH symptoms by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement.
Pygeum: Pygeum bark extract has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve urinary flow by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which can contribute to prostate hypertrophy.

Cernilton: Cernilton, an extract from the flowers of the rye grass plant, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm the prostatic tissue, reducing symptoms such as frequent urination and nocturia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Barks: The barks contain phenolic compounds and terpenes that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in treating prostate-related issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tea leaves of camellia sinensis and dry them properly for future use.
Combine 20 grams of dried tea leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler for infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to extract active compounds from the tea leaves into the oil.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of coconut oil for emulsification.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 20 minutes to melt the wax and create a smooth, spreadable cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eggplant creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain a unique compound called nasunin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve urinary flow in men suffering from BPH. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in eggplant extracts also help to relax the smooth muscle in the prostate gland, reducing symptoms such as frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, eggplant creams may help to break down prostatic tissue, allowing for easier passage of urine and reducing pressure on the bladder.
remedy_properties: Saw Palmetto (Serenoa repens): Has anti-inflammatory and estrogen-blocking properties, which may help reduce prostate enlargement and alleviate urinary symptoms by inhibiting the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT).
Beta-Sitosterol: A plant sterol that has been shown to have anti-androgenic effects, potentially reducing DHT levels and slowing down prostate cell growth.

Pygeum Africanum (Prunus africana): Contains flavonoids and terpenes that may help reduce inflammation, improve urinary flow, and decrease symptoms of BPH by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in alkaloids, which help relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Seeds: The seeds contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help protect the prostate gland from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 100g of dried solanum melongena into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground solanum melongena with 200ml of almond oil and heat at 70°c for 30 minutes to create an infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids after 15 minutes of pressure filtration.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the filtered solanum melongena infusion at room temperature until fully dissolved in 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle tissue in the prostate gland. The cream's active ingredients, such as maral root and saw palmetto, work together to improve urinary flow and alleviate symptoms of BPH, including frequent urination and difficulty starting or stopping the flow of urine. This natural remedy can help men manage their condition and enjoy a better quality of life without relying on pharmaceutical medications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair tree creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the prostate gland, which can contribute to BPH symptoms.

Musculotropic and spasmolytic properties: Maidenhair tree creams contain compounds that relax smooth muscle tissue, which can help alleviate urinary retention symptoms and improve urine flow in men with BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help shrink the enlarged prostate tissue.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 10ml of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass beaker at room temperature for 5 minutes.
Add 2.5ml of coconut oil, 1g of beeswax pellets, and 0.5g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until combined for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler set at 70°c for 15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely and the mixture has thickened slightly.
Remove the cream from heat, let it cool to 40°c, and transfer it to sterilized glass jars with tight-fitting lids for storage at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that specifically target the prostate gland. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense leaves in spring or summer for optimal bioactive compounds.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Combine 30g of dried equisetum arvense with 150ml of cold-pressed olive oil and infuse for 14 days.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and combine with 20g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil for emulsification.
Stir and heat the mixture at low temperature until the beeswax is melted and the cream reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it effectively alleviates urinary symptoms associated with the condition. The creams contain herbal extracts, such as Cuscuta chinensis and Cornus officinalis, which help to relax smooth muscles in the prostate gland and bladder neck, reducing obstruction and improving urine flow. Additionally, yanhusuo creams have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce swelling and irritation in the prostatic tissue, providing relief from frequent urination, weak stream, and other BPH symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cnidium Officinale Extract: Cnidium officinale extract is believed to help alleviate BPH symptoms by reducing inflammation, relaxing smooth muscles, and improving urine flow due to its anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects.

Curcuma Longa Extract (Turmeric): Curcumin, a polyphenol extracted from Turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce prostate swelling and alleviate BPH symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland.

Rhaponticum Carthamoides Extract: Rhapontic acid, a triterpenoid saponin extracted from Rhaponticum carthamoides, is thought to have anti-inflammatory and pro-apoptotic effects that may help reduce BPH symptoms by inhibiting prostate cell growth and inducing programmed cell death.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Yanhusuo plant contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to relax smooth muscle and reduce prostate swelling, making them ideal for relieving symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of corydalis yanhusuo roots in the shade for 7 days or until crispy.
Grind 50g of dried corydalis yanhusuo roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20g of ground corydalis yanhusuo with 80g of beeswax pellets and 40g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture reaches a temperature of 70°c.
Remove from heat, let cool to room temperature, then whip with an electric mixer for 10 minutes until creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barrenwort creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, particularly berberine, have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells and reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which can lead to symptom relief for men experiencing BPH. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Barrenwort creams contain flavonoids, which reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, helping to alleviate symptoms of BPH such as enlarged prostate and urinary tract discomfort.

Androgen receptor antagonist properties: The active compounds in Barrenwort creams, particularly icariin, have been shown to bind to androgen receptors, reducing the growth of prostatic cells and helping to relieve BPH symptoms like urinary frequency and hesitancy.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties: The antioxidants present in Barrenwort creams help protect the prostate gland from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of BPH.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality epimedium brevicornu flowers in the morning when they are fully open and dry them immediately.
Combine 20g of dried epimedium brevicornu with 100g of refined coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 1 hour to dissolve the beeswax and combine the ingredients, stirring occasionally to prevent scorching.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, squeezing gently to extract as much oil as possible from the epimedium brevicornu.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain active compounds that have a direct impact on the prostate gland. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh kava root extracts by simmering in water for one hour then drying at low temperature.
Grind dried kava root into fine powder using mortar and pestle before mixing with coconut oil and beeswax.
Combine ground kava root with 20% of its weight in coconut oil and 5% of its weight in beeswax then heat mixture at 70 degrees celsius for one hour.
Stir the cooled mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil per gram of cream.
Fill sterile glass jars with the final cream preparation after allowing it to cool and thicken fully for two hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because its key component, linalool, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms associated with an enlarged prostate. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant flavonoids present in lavender cream can help neutralize free radicals that can damage prostate tissue, potentially reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with BPH.

Musculotropic and sedative properties: Lavender's musculotropic effect (relaxing smooth muscle) may help alleviate urinary tract symptoms like frequency, urgency, and nocturia by relaxing the urethral sphincter and prostate gland, while its sedative properties can promote relaxation and reduce stress, which is often associated with BPH.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in a clean and well-ventilated area.
Grind 15 grams of dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered lavender with 25 grams of sweet almond oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler until melted and smooth.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 30 minutes, then filter through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar for 10 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before use, resulting in a soothing and calming balm.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for the recipe.
Combine 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers with 40 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut oil or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steam distill the mixture using a double boiler or steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oils for at least four hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a separate container and add 10 grams of beeswax pellets to create a solid cream base.
Melt the bee wax and essential oil mixture in a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl while stirring until the wax is fully incorporated and the cream has reached a smooth consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried panax ginseng roots 500 grams and grind into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine ground ginseng powder 250 grams with coconut oil 100 grams in a double boiler over low heat stirring constantly.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes or until the oil turns golden brown then remove from heat let cool.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and heat it over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids after 15 minutes of straining.
Combine the filtered liquid with 50 grams of cocoa butter and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, melting them over low heat for 45 minutes.

remedy_properties: Alpha-blocker activity: Mistletoe creams contain compounds that mimic the action of alpha-blockers, which help relax the muscles in the prostate and bladder neck, relieving symptoms of BPH such as urinary frequency, urgency, and hesitancy.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in mistletoe creams may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, which can contribute to the development and progression of BPH.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Mistletoe extracts have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and pain in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of BPH such as prostatic enlargement and lower urinary tract symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiprostate properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids, which have been demonstrated to have antioxidant and anti-edematous effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of enlarged prostate.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry viscum album leaves and flowers in a warm dark place for 7-10 days.
Grind 20 grams of dried viscum album into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered viscum album with 50 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 15-20 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 60°c to 70°c.
Strain the cooled cream through cheesecloth into sterile containers and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for benign prostatic hypertrophy are natural topical preparations containing botanical extracts that alleviate symptoms associated with enlarged prostate glands. These creams help by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and relaxing the prostate muscle, thereby relieving symptoms such as urinary frequency, urgency, and nocturia. Examples of herbal creams include Saw Palmetto, Pygeum, and Stinging Nettle creams, which have been shown to improve urinary flow rates and reduce prostatic symptoms in studies. By using these natural creams, men can experience improved sleep quality, reduced frequency of bathroom trips, and enhanced overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy with herbal creams is by using a combination of ingredients that target inflammation and relaxation of the prostate. Saw palmetto, stinging nettle, and pygeum are popular herbal extracts that have been shown to reduce symptoms such as enlarged prostate, frequent urination, and painful urination. Look for creams containing these herbs in conjunction with other natural ingredients like aloe vera and vitamin E to provide optimal relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to benign prostatic hypertrophy that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as menopausal symptoms, vaginal dryness, and urinary incontinence. Herbal creams containing ingredients like soy isoflavones, black cohosh, and wild yam have been used to alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal atrophy. These natural remedies may also help reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, providing relief for women experiencing menopause-related symptoms.
